factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Escalation
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran for second straight day, Iran retaliatory missile attacks on Bahrain/Kuwait/Qatar, ceasefire collapsed. Oil surged 7% to $74.50+, raising inflation risk and energy cost concerns for equities.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Cost Inflation Risk
factors.summary: WTI extended gains above $74 on Strait of Hormuz disruption fears and Trump's threat to target Kharg Island. Higher energy costs compress tech margins and risk Fed hold/hike bias if inflation re-accelerates.

factors.name: China CPI Disappointment
factors.summary: China CPI fell to 1.0% YoY in June vs 1.1% expected; PPI surged to 4.1% 4-year high. Two-track economy (weak domestic demand, strong export pricing) signals demand headwinds for tech hardware and cloud infrastructure.

factors.name: Chipmaker Strength & AI Optimism
factors.summary: SK Hynix US listing 7x+ oversubscribed; Broadcom-Apple supply deal and reports of limited Nvidia H200 China approval lifted semiconductor sentiment. Nvidia BofA note argues valuation anomaly at 11-year PE low vs peers.

factors.name: Real Yields & Duration Risk
factors.summary: Gold dropped to $4,075 amid oil spike and renewed inflation/rate-hike fears from Trump's Iran escalation rhetoric. Long-duration growth multiples vulnerable if nominal rates re-anchor higher.

factors.name: Fed Messaging (FOMC Minutes Context)
factors.summary: FOMC minutes noted 'a few' participants saw case for rate raises. Combined with oil inflation risk, this tilts toward hawkish hold bias; no imminent cut expectations.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Calendar
factors.summary: No mega-cap earnings announced in this window. Next catalysts likely Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia in coming weeks; earnings season is main NDX alpha driver.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Valuation
factors.summary: Oil surge and geopolitical risk typically support USD near-term; strong dollar headwind for tech multinationals' foreign revenue and multiple expansion.

factors.name: Sentiment & Positioning
factors.summary: Bitcoin tumbled to $62K support; crypto weakness often signals risk-off rotation. Retail SPX/NDX enthusiasm was crowded long on USD strength; geopolitical shock may flush complacency.

factors.name: Price Action Context (4h/24h)
factors.summary: NAS100 up only +0.12% in 4h and +0.60% in 24h despite oil+7%, Iran war, China miss. Weak breadth suggests rally is stalling; move not extended enough to confirm phase-3 but lack of conviction is red flag.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught in a tug-of-war between chipmaker/AI strength (SK Hynix IPO hype, Broadcom-Apple deal) and escalating macro headwinds (US-Iran war restart, oil +7%, China growth miss, real yield re-acceleration). The index is up only 0.6% over 24h despite headline risk, signaling weak conviction and likely consolidation or pullback near resistance. Geopolitical shock could unmask crowded long positioning if oil holds above $74 and triggers inflation repricing.
catalysts_ahead: Oil supply disruption updates from Strait of Hormuz / Trump Iran rhetoric
China stimulus response or fiscal data (likely weak given June CPI miss)
FOMC participants' hawkish commentary on rate hikes
Mega-cap earnings guidance over next 2-3 weeks (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL)
Real yields & 10Y Treasury move if oil/inflation premium persists
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if (1) Iran-US ceasefire suddenly restored and oil crashes below $72, (2) Fed explicitly signals cut cycle ahead, (3) chipmaker/hyperscaler earnings surprise to upside with strong forward guidance, (4) China announces major fiscal stimulus (rare given local-govt austerity). Conversely, further oil rally past $76 and/or Trump targeting Kharg Island would confirm BEARISH and trigger sharp selloff in duration longs.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in progress but weak breadth (flat 24h price vs. headline risk) suggests no conviction yet. Wait for retest of 29,050–29,150 support before considering short; alternatively, wait for clarity on Iran ceasefire status and oil hold above/below $74 before adding long. Avoid chasing highs into earnings season with macro this fragmented.

factors.name: US-Iran Escalation / Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran overnight, Iranian retaliatory ballistic missiles on Bahrain/Kuwait/Jordan bases, Strait of Hormuz traffic near-halted. This is a genuine supply disruption risk that historically weighs on growth equities and compressed multiples.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike / Energy Cost Inflation
factors.summary: WTI holding near $74-75.75, Goldman warns Hormuz flare-up may delay oil supply recovery. Rising energy costs feed into inflation narrative and squeeze profit margins for tech/growth names with thin real yields.

factors.name: China Economic Data Mixed / Deflation vs Producer Inflation
factors.summary: China June CPI came in at 1.0% YoY (vs 1.2% expected, prior 1.2%), but PPI spiked to 4.1% YoY (4-year high). Weak consumer demand + rising input costs = margin squeeze in China, dampening semiconductor and capex demand signals.

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty / Real Yields Pivot
factors.summary: Fed Meeting Minutes Wednesday sparked USD weakness as markets repriced rate expectations lower. AI inflation fears offset dovish signals per Fed policymakers. Longer-duration NDX names sensitive to real yield moves; uncertainty persists on peak rates.

factors.name: Semiconductor / AI Capex Sentiment
factors.summary: SK Hynix US ADR $28B listing heavily oversubscribed, Broadcom-Apple supply deal, Nvidia $500M Australian cloud investment, reports China may allow limited Nvidia H200 sales. Chip demand constructive despite macro headwinds.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness vs DXY
factors.summary: USD/DXY near 101.00, pressured for second straight day on Fed repricing and Iran risk. Weaker dollar typically headwind for US equities with heavy international revenue; also reduces real yield pressure temporarily but adds FX volatility.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings / Forward Guidance Void
factors.summary: No major earnings or hyperscaler capex guidance in the news window. Levi's Q2 beat but guided weak, other mega-caps silent. Absence of bullish catalyst limits upside conviction.

factors.name: Macro Volatility / Market Consolidation
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.12% 4h, +0.60% 24h—minimal directional progress despite headline volatility. Market consolidating after recent Trump de-escalation bounce. Lack of conviction breakout amid geopolitical and policy uncertainty.

factors.name: Bank of Japan Rate Hike Signal / Yen Intervention Risk
factors.summary: BoJ officials warning of potential rate hikes above 2%, USD/JPY near 162.00s (4-decade high). Yen strength could trigger intervention, add volatility to risk assets and complicate Fed/growth narrative.

factors.name: VIX / Risk Asset Positioning
factors.summary: Overnight volatility from Iran escalation present, but not reflected in extreme positioning data in news. Oil, crypto, and equities holding steadier than expected; risk-off not yet severe but lingering caution evident.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is consolidating after Trump's initial de-escalation bounce, now facing renewed US-Iran military clashes that threaten oil supply and feed inflation fears. Fed policy uncertainty (dovish repricing after Wednesday minutes) clashes with AI capex upside and geopolitical downside—a classic PHASE_2 tug-of-war. Semiconductor demand signals remain constructive (SK Hynix listing, Broadcom-Apple, China H200), but China's weak CPI (1.0% vs 1.2%) and soaring PPI (4.1%) signal margin pressure and deflationary domestic demand. The real yield picture is uncertain: weaker dollar and Fed repricing support growth equities, but rising oil and BoJ hike signals reintroduce real yield headwinds. Markets are pricing caution into the close of trading.
catalysts_ahead: US weekly Initial Jobless Claims (Thursday AM US time)
Bank of Korea rate decision next Thursday (hike now widely expected)
Fed speakers / further Powell/Barkin commentary on AI inflation
Iran developments and Strait of Hormuz shipping updates (real-time)
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN) in coming weeks
FOMC minutes, PCE inflation data (critical for duration risk)
China PBoC policy response to weak CPI
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US escalation contains quickly and oil rolls over below $72, or if Fed signals faster rate cuts, NAS100 could rally 1-2% into resistance. Conversely, if Hormuz traffic remains disrupted, oil spikes above $78, or geopolitical risk premia persist, consolidation breaks lower to 29000–28950. Earnings surprises from mega-caps (especially NVDA, MSFT cloud guidance) would override macro backdrop; absence of such catalysts leaves bias vulnerable to mean-reversion chop.
screening.entry_guidance: Consolidation-bound; avoid chasing into resistance at 29350–29400. Consider nibbling long breakout above 29400 only if oil stabilizes sub-$74 and Iran tensions fade. For shorts, wait for retest of 29200–29250 with confirmed rejection of 29350 resistance before scaling in on risk-off acceleration.
